justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classification (10 rules total, exceeding minimum). Notification rule (BR-10) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions screening (BR-7) included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as a critical gap. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 78% falls within the 60-85 range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified plus 3 additional rules (unverified withdrawal, unverified receive, support-only unlock) — comprehensive coverage
Notification rule correctly marked invariant: false with clear reasoning ('failure to notify does not block the transaction')
Sanctions screening rule included as invariant: true with strong testable assertion
Timezone ambiguity explicitly called out as 'critical ambiguity' in edge cases and confidence assessment
12 acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format — triple the minimum requirement
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified ($500) from unverified (cannot send) limits without conflating them
Edge cases section is exceptional — covers race conditions, mid-day KYC upgrade, structuring risk, and pre-settlement reversal
Confidence level of 78% is well-calibrated given the ambiguities identified
Business impact and stakeholder analysis adds genuine domain authority beyond the minimum requirements
Identifies additional real gaps: partial verification entry criteria, notification delivery guarantees, concurrent request atomicity

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
This encodes the core transactional and compliance lifecycle of a digital wallet product. The business process spans identity verification (KYC), money movement (send/receive/withdraw), fraud prevention (account locking), regulatory compliance (sanctions screening, transfer limits), and user communication (notifications).
